name: Trail to the Lakes of Covadonga
name_original: Lagos de Covadonga – ida y vuelta por el Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa
description: Cycle the difficult 28.3-mile Lagos de Covadonga route through Picos de Europa National Park, gaining 4402 feet to reach glacial lakes.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Cangas De Onís traverse a landscape characterized by the dramatic karstic terrain of the Picos de Europa National Park, featuring significant elevation changes and challenging climbs. The region is situated on the banks of the Sella River, with routes often leading through verdant valleys and extensive native forests. Cyclists encounter demanding ascents, including routes with over 1,500 feet of elevation gain, making it a destination for those seeking challenging rides. This area serves as a gateway to high mountain passes and glacial lakes, offering varied road cycling terrain. Best road cycling routes around Cangas De Onís The most popular road cycling route is Trail to the Lakes of Covadonga, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This route features an epic climb to the legendary Lagos de Covadonga. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís, a difficult 51.3 miles (82.6 km) path. This route includes significant ascents and passes through mythical mountain terrain. Local road cyclists also love the Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves, a 49.6 miles (79.9 km) trail leading through the rolling green meadows and stunning alpine beauty of the Picos de Europa, often completed in about 4 hours 40 minutes. Road cycling around Cangas De Onís is defined by the dramatic Picos de Europa National Park, the serene Lakes of Covadonga, and the Sella River valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Cangas De Onís are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Cangas De Onís's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Cangas de Onís?
A: Road cycling routes in Cangas de Onís primarily traverse the dramatic karstic terrain of the Picos de Europa National Park. You can expect significant elevation changes and challenging climbs, with many routes featuring ascents exceeding 1,500 feet. The region also offers rides through verdant valleys and extensive native forests along the Sella River.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Cangas de Onís?
A: While Cangas de Onís is known for its challenging climbs, there are a few routes suitable for beginners or those seeking an easier ride. The komoot community has identified 2 easy routes among the total of 28 available. These routes generally feature less elevation gain and shorter distances, providing a gentler introduction to the region's beautiful landscapes.

Q: What are some of the most challenging road cycling climbs in the area?
A: Cangas de Onís is a prime destination for challenging road cycling. A notable and demanding route is the epic climb from Soto de Cangas to the legendary Lagos de Covadonga, which spans 19 km with an impressive 1,129 meters of elevation gain. Other routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078480886/balconies-of-picos-de-europa-loop-from-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís&lt;/a&gt;, include significant ascents and pass through mythical mountain terrain, catering to cyclists with very good fitness.

Q: What natural features can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of the Picos de Europa National Park, a well-conserved biosphere reserve. You can encounter dramatic gorges, pristine rivers like the Sella, and extensive native forests. The serene glacial &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/195967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Ercina&lt;/a&gt; and Lake Enol are also prominent features on routes leading to Covadonga, offering stunning alpine beauty.

Q: Are there any iconic landmarks or viewpoints accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, several iconic landmarks and viewpoints are accessible. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067413747/trail-to-the-lakes-of-covadonga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to the Lakes of Covadonga&lt;/a&gt; leads to the famous Lakes of Covadonga, offering breathtaking vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves&lt;/a&gt; also provides spectacular views of the rolling green meadows and alpine scenery. Additionally, the Roman Bridge in Cangas de Onís itself is a significant architectural landmark often seen at the start or end of routes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Cangas de Onís?
A: The best time for road cycling in Cangas de Onís is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and the landscapes are particularly vibrant. Summer can also be excellent, though some climbs might be more challenging in higher temperatures. Winter cycling is possible but may be limited by snow and colder conditions, especially at higher elevations within the Picos de Europa.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Cangas de Onís offers several circular road cycling routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1078480886/balconies-of-picos-de-europa-loop-from-cangas-de-onis" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balconies of Picos de Europa Loop from Cangas de Onís&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24634612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Queen's Viewpoint – Lagos de Covadonga loop from Cueves&lt;/a&gt; are popular options that bring you back to your starting point, allowing for a comprehensive exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Is public transport available for cyclists in Cangas de Onís, especially for accessing routes in Picos de Europa?
A: Cangas de Onís serves as a gateway to the Picos de Europa National Park. While specific public transport options for bikes directly into the park's higher reaches can be limited or seasonal, local bus services connect Cangas de Onís to key areas. It's advisable to check local transport schedules and bike carriage policies in advance, especially for routes like Lagos de Covadonga, which has restricted vehicle access during peak seasons.

Q: Are there any permits or special regulations for cycling in the Picos de Europa National Park?
A: The Picos de Europa National Park is a protected area. While general road cycling on public roads typically doesn't require specific permits, certain areas, especially the ascent to Lagos de Covadonga, have seasonal restrictions on private vehicle access. Cyclists are generally allowed, but it's always recommended to respect park regulations, stay on designated routes, and be aware of environmental protection rules. There are no specific cycling permits mentioned for general road use.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about the routes in Cangas de Onís?
A: The road cycling routes around Cangas de Onís are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 30 reviews. Over 5,000 road cyclists have explored the varied terrain using komoot, often praising the challenging climbs, stunning mountain scenery, and the rewarding views, particularly towards the Lakes of Covadonga.

Q: Are there any cafes or accommodation options along the cycling routes?
A: Cangas de Onís itself offers all the necessary amenities for tourists, including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation, making it an ideal base. Along some of the more popular routes, especially those leading towards Covadonga, you may find establishments catering to visitors. However, in more remote mountain areas, services can be sparse, so it's wise to plan your stops and carry sufficient supplies.
tips: For me one of the most beautiful and challenging climbs in the region. Perfect roads, only buses and approved taxis are allowed on the route. The view on the way and, of course, the one when you have reached the top compensate everyone who has undertaken this effort. At the top of the lake there is a small bar where you can eat and drink something at normal prices. And not to forget the grandiose descent on a perfect asphalt road 😱😉😊 .... definitely visit the basilica

The views from Mirador de la Reina are absolutely stunning. You can see the surrounding mountains and valleys, as well as the winding road that leads to the viewpoint. Due to its popularity, the viewpoint can get very crowded during the peak tourist season. It is best to arrive early in the morning or later in the afternoon to avoid the crowds.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Lagos de Covadonga cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as an "HC" (Hors Catégorie) climb, meaning it's extremely difficult and requires very good fitness. It features an average gradient of 6.7% to 8.2% over 12.3 to 19 kilometers, but this is deceptive. You'll encounter sections like "La Huesera" with gradients of 14-15% and peaks up to 16%, making it a truly demanding ascent.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on the climb to Lagos de Covadonga?
faqs.answer: The road surface on the primary climb to Lagos de Covadonga is generally in very good condition, making it suitable for road bikes. However, the gradients are notoriously inconsistent, with steep ramps, short descents, and flatter sections. The route transitions from shaded forest roads in its initial stages to more exposed alpine sections as you ascend.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: Beyond the stunning Picos de Europa National Park scenery, key highlights include the challenging Climb to Lagos de Covadonga from Cangas de Onís itself, and the panoramic views from the Queen's Viewpoint. At the summit, you'll be rewarded with the spectacular glacial lakes, Lake Enol and Lake Ercina.

faqs.question: Where can I park, and are there any traffic restrictions for cyclists heading to Lagos de Covadonga?
faqs.answer: During peak seasons (typically July and August), private cars are often prohibited from accessing the climb to the lakes, which significantly enhances the cycling experience by reducing traffic. You would typically park in Cangas de Onís or Covadonga and start your ride from there. Shuttle buses operate to transport tourists to the lakes during these periods.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle to Lagos de Covadonga?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During peak summer months (July and August), car traffic is often restricted, making for a more pleasant cycling experience. Always check the weather forecast, as conditions in the mountains can change rapidly.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cyclists to access the Lagos de Covadonga climb within the Picos de Europa National Park. However, it's important to respect national park regulations, such as staying on marked paths, not disturbing wildlife, and leaving no trace.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Picos de Europa National Park generally allows dogs, specific rules apply. Dogs must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and livestock. Given the demanding nature of this particular cycling route, it might not be suitable for all dogs, especially if you plan to cycle the entire distance with them. Consider the heat and the strenuous climb.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The full round trip, covering approximately 45.6 km with over 1340 meters of elevation gain, typically takes around 3 to 4 hours for a fit cyclist. However, this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make for photos, and the weather conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known cycling or hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. Notably, it covers a significant portion of the official cycling climb [CIMA AS07] Lagos de Covadonga. It also intersects with parts of the Ruta Andariega (Gijón / Xixón - Covadonga/Cuadonga), Ruta del Rey Favila, and the PR-PNPE 2 Lagos de Covadonga (variante corta).

faqs.question: What makes the Lagos de Covadonga climb so famous among cyclists?
faqs.answer: The Lagos de Covadonga is arguably Spain's most cherished cycling climb, a recurring and often decisive stage in the prestigious Vuelta a España Grand Tour. Its fame comes from its extreme difficulty, exceptional natural beauty within the Picos de Europa National Park, and its tactical challenge due to constantly changing gradients, preventing riders from settling into a steady rhythm.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the route?
faqs.answer: You'll find cafes and restaurants in Cangas de Onís and Covadonga at the base of the climb. There are also some facilities near the lakes at the summit, though these can be busy, especially during peak season. It's always wise to carry enough water and snacks for the climb itself.

waypoints.name: Climb to Lagos de Covadonga from Cangas de Onís
